title: Review Project Onboarding Feature Readiness
description: Review the combined packaging, onboarding, testing, and documentation changes for correctness, portability, and alignment with the spec's reusable-project goal.
acceptance_criteria: Review confirms the implementation meets the stated acceptance criteria without relying on self-hosting-only paths.
Review calls out any residual risk around deferred decisions such as skill allowlist configurability or upgrade flows.
Review signs off only after shared test and documentation follow-up beads are complete.

handoff_summary.completed: Both dependency beads are done. The corrective bead (B-d58d5f7e-corrective) fixed the only genuine error: beads/ directory label now correctly reads 'version-controlled' instead of 'gitignored'. The gitignore strategy in docs/onboarding.md (4 explicit exclusions) is accurate and matches _GITIGNORE_ENTRIES in onboarding.py. The previous review's Finding 1 was a mischaracterization — the docs were correct all along. tests/test_cli_init.py covers all 11 scenarios with correct patch targets.
handoff_summary.risks: Deferred, non-blocking: upgrade path for bundled templates/skills is not documented. Skill allowlist configurability is mentioned in Post-Init section but not marked as a formally deferred decision per AC2. Neither is a blocker.
handoff_summary.next_action: No further action required. Bead can be marked complete.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The actual .gitignore strategy (4 explicit exclusions) in onboarding.py matches the documentation exactly. The self-hosting repo uses a different .orchestrator/* catch-all approach in its own .gitignore, but that is separate from what onboarding.py generates for new projects.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: tests/test_cli_init.py covers all 11 scenarios with correct patch targets (codex_orchestrator.onboarding.scaffold_project, codex_orchestrator.onboarding.collect_init_answers). No gaps.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Upgrade path for bundled templates/skills not documented (deferred). Skill allowlist configurability noted but not formally marked as deferred per AC2.

execution_history.summary: Review finds 2 documentation errors in docs/onboarding.md: the gitignore strategy shown does not match the actual implementation (catch-all .orchestrator/* with negations vs. 4 explicit exclusions), and beads/ is incorrectly labeled as gitignored when it is version-controlled. Tests are solid. Verdict: needs_changes.
Created corrective bead B-d58d5f7e-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B-d58d5f7e-corrective completed
Approved after corrective bead fixed beads/ label; gitignore documentation is accurate and matches implementation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review only the changed files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read unrelated files.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.
- Focus on correctness, completeness, and risk — not style or formatting.
- Keep the review concise. If there are no findings, say so and approve promptly.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (reviews always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of the review result.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the review signoff decision: `approved` means the bead can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when no unresolved findings remain.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved finding count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when any required fix remains.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.

worker_prompt_context.title: Review Project Onboarding Feature Readiness
worker_prompt_context.description: Review the combined packaging, onboarding, testing, and documentation changes for correctness, portability, and alignment with the spec's reusable-project goal.
acceptance_criteria: Review confirms the implementation meets the stated acceptance criteria without relying on self-hosting-only paths.
Review calls out any residual risk around deferred decisions such as skill allowlist configurability or upgrade flows.
Review signs off only after shared test and documentation follow-up beads are complete.
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: No file-scope conflicts with other in-progress beads. Only docs/onboarding.md requires correction.

handoff_summary.completed: Both dependency beads (B-aeb8ca9f: tests done, B-83f9c770: docs done) are in done status. AC3 satisfied. Test file covers all stated scenarios correctly. Patch targets are consistent with the handoff design decision.
handoff_summary.remaining: docs/onboarding.md must be corrected to reflect the actual .gitignore strategy (.orchestrator/* with negations) and remove the incorrect "gitignored" label from beads/ in the directory tree. A documentation-only fix is required before this bead can be approved.
handoff_summary.risks: If users follow the documented gitignore approach instead of the actual implementation, bead state (beads/) would not be tracked in git, breaking team collaboration. agent-runs/ and other transient outputs would also be unguarded. Additionally, no upgrade path is documented for when bundled templates/skills change in future package versions.
handoff_summary.next_action: Fix docs/onboarding.md: (1) Replace the 4-entry gitignore block with the actual .orchestrator/* catch-all plus negation pattern approach. (2) Correct the beads/ directory comment from "runtime, gitignored" to reflect that it is tracked in git.
handoff_summary.block_reason: docs/onboarding.md documents an incorrect .gitignore strategy (4 explicit exclusions) that does not match the real implementation (.orchestrator/* catch-all with negations for beads/ and config.yaml). Additionally, beads/ is mislabeled as gitignored when it is version-controlled. These errors would mislead users setting up new projects and cause bead state loss in team scenarios.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No file-scope conflicts with other in-progress beads. Only docs/onboarding.md requires correction.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The actual .gitignore uses .orchestrator/* with negations (!.orchestrator/beads/, !.orchestrator/beads/**, !.orchestrator/config.yaml) to keep bead state and config tracked while excluding transient runtime artifacts. The documentation must reflect this approach, not a simpler 4-entry explicit exclusion list.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: tests/test_cli_init.py covers all 11 scenarios described in the file header. Patch targets (codex_orchestrator.onboarding.scaffold_project, codex_orchestrator.onboarding.collect_init_answers) are correct per the handoff note about local imports in command_init. No test gaps identified.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Upgrade path for bundled templates/skills is not documented (deferred risk, not a blocker). Skill allowlist configurability is mentioned in Post-Init section but not documented as a deferred decision per AC2.

last_agent_result.summary: Approved after corrective bead fixed beads/ label; gitignore documentation is accurate and matches implementation.
